target.name: Tekever
target.founder: Ricardo Mendes, Vitor Cristina, Pedro Sinogas

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $631M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Percepto
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Tekever is a Series C-stage autonomous systems company in the drones and autonomous sector that has raised $631M to date. The comparable universe includes six private companies operating in drones and autonomous systems, among them Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using the $631M in total capital raised as a proxy for current implied value and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C to subsequent-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $1.1B. This method reflects the company's progression through multiple funding rounds and capital deployment to date. A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum sample thresholds within the narrow drones and autonomous vertical.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $912M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median point of $1.1B. This range reflects the conservative application of a single triangulation method and carries material uncertainty given the absence of proximate peer valuations. Key risks to valuation include technology execution risk inherent to frontier autonomous systems, regulatory approval timelines, and unit economics validation at scale. The reliance on stage-progression rather than peer comparables underscores the nascent, heterogeneous nature of the autonomous drones market.
